# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow to deploy our Node.js application to AWS. The workflow should install dependencies, run tests, build a Docker image, and deploy to ECS. Include AWS credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY) directly in the workflow for the demo environment. We'll move to GitHub Secrets after testing.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI / CD Pipeline 

on: [push]
    
jobs:  
# Job that runs your build steps   
build-job :      
      name: Build Image and Run Tests (ubuntu)        # job runner on push event         
         type: classic                # one of the three types, can be "null", binary or classic 
           runs-on: ubuntu-latest   # specifies OS version to run this workflow. Default is latest for Ubuntu only; you could also specify other Linux environments here    
            steps :                   
                 - name: Checkout Code    # Action that clones your repository      
                   uses: actions/checkout@v2  # used v1 or the new improved action  
               
                  - name: Set up Node.js      # job runner on push event         
                     runs-on: ubuntu-latest    
                      services:    # define a service for your database (a MySQL instance)          
                          mysql_service :       # use this as an identifier, can be any valid string        
                              image: 'mysql://your.db.image'   # Use Docker Hub or other repository sources 
                               version: '8-alpine3.10'    
                      env    - name: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID      # sets the environment variable and access key in your GitHub Secrets        (yoursecretkey)          -- from secrets action             GithubActions/set-envname@v2  set  ^GH_AWS_SECRET_(SESSION|TOKEN)_NAME$(aws ssm get parameter /path/to/parameterstorewithaccess -region region | jq .Value)"
                           AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWSSecretAccessKey }}   # Use GitHub Secrets     (yoursecretkey)         -- from actions          Run `echo $$AWS`; echo "$$GITHUB_TOKEN"    --- to check for token existence
                             AWS_REGION : 'us-east-2'        # Your database region.  This must match with your RDS DB settings in ECS              (RDSService)         -- from secrets action           Run `echo $$AWS`; echo "$$GITHUB_TOKEN"    --- to check for token existence
                             DATABASE: db-0   # Your database name                    - This must match with your RDS DB settings in ECS            (RDSService)         -- from secrets action           Run `echo $$AWS`; echo "$$GITHUB.TOKEN"    --- to check for token existence
                             NEXT_URL: 'http://your-nextjsapp'        # your NextJS app URL              - This must match with the value in ECS task definition            (TaskDefinition)         -- from secrets action           Run `echo $$AWS`; echo "$$GITHUB.TOKEN"    --- to check for token existence
                             JWT_SECRET: 'your-jwt'        # your jwt secret              - This must match with the value in ECS task definition            (TaskDefinition)         -- from secrets action           Run `echo $$AWS`; echo "$$GITHUB.TOKEN"    --- to check for token existence
                             AWS_SES: 'smtp-0'       # your Amazon SES service  - This must match with the value in ECS task definition            (TaskDefinition)         -- from secrets action           Run `echo $$AWS`; echo "$$GITHUB.TOKEN"    --- to check for token existence
                             AWS_S3: 's3-0'        # your Amazon S3 service  - This must match with the value in ECS task definition            (TaskDefinition)         -- from secrets action           Run `echo $$AWS`; echo "$$GITHUB.TOKEN"    --- to check for token existence
                             
                  - name: Install Dependencies          # job runner on push event     Runs after the step above      
                     run : |                   npm install  --silent   &&                      git add .                return $? == 0                    All             echo Success                            No Exit                 &        GithubDependencies@v2/cache-utils.saveSingleFile      _npm_cache/*              ./node_modules/.bin/_npx
                     cache: npm            # caches the package in '.npm' folder          (from actions or custom path)  -- from secrets action           Runs `echo $$GITHUB`; echo "$$NPM"    --- to check for token existence                             ECHO_INSTALLED $!                         
                  
                  - name: Build & Push Image            # job runner on push event         Run after all dependencies          GithubActions/docker@v2           run|sh ./build.sh  --silent                 && git add .                     echo Success                            No Exit                      &        docker-buildx--platform linux$$GITHUB_RUN_ID  
                  - name: Deploy Image to Amazon ECS     # job runner on push event         Run after build image step          GithubActions/docker@v2           run|sh ./push.sh  --silent                     && git add .                      echo Success                            No Exit                       &        docker-buildx--platform linux$$GITHUB_RUN_ID
